1.name: Chupa Chups (Spain)
1.text: The logo of the brand was created by Salvador Dalí (Spanish painter) in 1969

2.name: Fish and Chips (United Kingdom)
2.text: The British governament safeguarded supplies of the dish's ingredients during the First World War.

3.name: Tatin pie (France)
3.text: Apple pie created in 1880s at Hotel Tatin by two sisters Stéphanie and Caroline Tatin

4.name: Tempura (Japan)
4.text: Japanese adaptation of the portuguese dish “peixinhos da horta”

5.name: Mandioca (Africa) (cassava in English)
5.text: It is used in pasta dishes especially in African cultures.

6.name: Quesadilla (Mexico)
6.text: Originaly from colonial Mexico (16th century) as a fusion between corn tortillas and dairy products like cheese.

1.name: Burj Khalifa (United Arab Emirates)
1.text: It is the tallest building in the world with 829,8m of height located in United Arab Emirates

2.name: Great wall of China
2.text: It is a fortification built across northen China from 7th century BC to protect from nomadic invasions.

3.name: Taj Mahal (India)
3.text: It was constructed featuring Persian, Islamic and Indian styles. This is a UNESCO World Heritage Site

4.name: Statue of Liberty (USA)
4.text: It is a universal symbol of freedom, democracy and friendship which was offered to U.S. on October 28, 1886 by France.

5.name: Ruins of St. Pauls
5.text: It  is a 17th century facade of a former Jesuit church in Macau, destroyed by fire in 1835.

6.name: Tower Bridge (United Kingdom)
6.text: It was constructed between 1886 and 1894 in London.
